description:
Apply pending C# edits to the running Unity Editor in ~1-2 seconds via method hot-patching — no script recompile, no domain reload, no state loss. Works in Edit Mode and Play Mode; ideal for the edit→observe loop while playing.

Use after editing .cs files with the write/edit tools. Edits are classified automatically:
- Hot (applied live): method/property/indexer/operator/constructor BODY changes (conversion operators only FROM the declaring type), async↔sync conversion, using-directive changes (the whole file re-binds), accessibility widening, new methods/properties/indexers/events of ANY declared accessibility (accessor members materialize as get_/set_/add_/remove_ shims; auto-properties virtualize their backing field like added fields; a few call-site shapes stay cold with a pointed reason: object initializers, ++/--, ref/out, deconstruction targets, ?. access, value-consumed or side-effect-receiver compound assignments) — added-member bodies may only touch PUBLIC pre-existing surface (or surface added in the same session) unless the runtime access probe measured non-public access green (then private/internal references go hot too); non-public types in the member's SIGNATURE stay cold — class field add/remove/retype (virtualized: existing instances read default(T) for new fields, new instances run initializers, Inspector shows them only after a real compile), new types, new .cs files.
- Hot after call-site verification: method signature/return-type/static changes, renames, member deletions, accessibility narrowing — verified by scanning compiled call sites; if calls exist OUTSIDE this edit batch the tool returns cold with the EXACT files to edit (then retry). Deleting a Unity message method (Update/...) stops its behavior immediately via an empty stub.
- Cold (needs a real compile): field reorders/attribute changes, struct or generic-type fields, base-type/attribute changes, generic method bodies, interface members, partial-type FIELD/initializer changes and types with source-generator parts (hand-written partial body & member edits ARE hot — sibling part files join the batch automatically), NEW Unity message methods, virtual-member signature changes, constructor surface changes, const value changes, operator/conversion additions, field-like (accessor-less) event additions, virtual member additions, conversions returning their declaring type. The tool reports the exact reasons and queues the files — then call unity_recompile. Edits work across asmdef assemblies (callers and callees may live in different assemblies), but .asmdef files themselves are never hot-reload inputs — adding/editing/deleting an assembly definition restructures assemblies and always needs unity_recompile.
- Blind spots: reflection by name, SendMessage(string), UnityEvent serialized bindings cannot be verified — they keep calling old members until a real compile.

After many patches, an idle stretch, or leaving Play Mode, Locus runs a silent convergence recompile automatically — hot state and disk truth then re-align.

Deterministic compile errors are returned directly; fix the code and call again. Hot patches persist until the next domain reload, and the edited files are already on disk, so a later unity_recompile (or any reload) converges to identical code permanently — call unity_recompile once you finish a burst of hot edits, before relying on serialization/Inspector changes, or whenever the tool tells you to.

Requires: Unity hot reload + sidecar compiler enabled in settings, an up-to-date Locus Unity plugin, and Editor Code Optimization = Debug.

paths.description: Optional .cs paths (absolute or project-relative) to hot reload. Default: every .cs file edited since the last recompile.
